chapter_title: Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
text:
The commission may authorize a port authority to issue permits for the movement of oversize or overweight vehicles carrying cargo on state highways located in counties:

(1) contiguous to the Gulf of Mexico or a bay or inlet opening into the gulf and:

(A) adjacent to at least two counties with a population of 550,000 or more; or

(B) bordering the United Mexican States; or

(2) contiguous to the Gulf of Mexico or a bay or inlet opening into the gulf with a population of not more than 200,000 and adjacent to a county described by Subdivision (1)(A).
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 623. Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
##### § 623.212 — Permits by Port Authority

The commission may authorize a port authority to issue permits for the movement of oversize or overweight vehicles carrying cargo on state highways located in counties:

    (1) contiguous to the Gulf of Mexico or a bay or inlet opening into the gulf and:

        (A) adjacent to at least two counties with a population of 550,000 or more; or

        (B) bordering the United Mexican States; or

    (2) contiguous to the Gulf of Mexico or a bay or inlet opening into the gulf with a population of not more than 200,000 and adjacent to a county described by Subdivision (1)(A).

*Acts 2017, 85th Leg., R.S., Ch. 177 (H.B.4156), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2017.*
